political.s: FEC: $307K in executive political donations; partisan lean split across 11 executive donors.

charity.s: $4M reported corporate giving in 2024 (~0.06% of revenue) via JCPenney Communities Foundation.
charity.focus: Youth education and opportunity, food security, emergency relief, diversity support

labor.s: 1 OSHA severe-injury reports in past 2y (0 amputations); 5 since 2015.

guns.s: No federal firearms license on record for this company in the ATF FFL registry — no licensed firearms dealing or manufacturing.

wiki.extract: JCPenney is an American department store chain founded in 1902 by James Cash Penney. It is owned by Catalyst Brands, a joint venture between Authentic Brands Group, Brookfield Corporation, Shein, and Simon Property Group, since 2025.

cases.natureOfSuit: Civil Rights: Americans with Disabilities - Other

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy

health.s: 11 CPSC recalls on record; sample hazard: the children's nightgowns fail to meet the flammability standards for children's.

topHazards.label: The children's nightgowns fail to meet the flammability standards for children's sleepwear, posing a risk of burn injuries to children.
The puffer jackets have a drawstring located inside the lining of the bottom area of the garment. The drawstring can become entangled or caught on playground s…
The patches on the shorts can detach, posing a choking hazard to young children.

sampleRecalls.title: Children's Sleepwear Recalled Due to Violation of Federal Flammability Standards and Burn Hazard; Imported by Jammers Apparel Group; Sold Exclusively at JCPenney
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Dots & Dreams Dollie & Me-branded children's nightgowns made of 100% polyester. The short-sleeved nightgowns were sold in rainbow print on a mint-green background and have "WKND" screen-printed on the front of the garment. They were sold in five sizes (XXS, XS, S, M, L) and were accompanied by a m…
hazards: The children's nightgowns fail to meet the flammability standards for children's sleepwear, posing a risk of burn injuries to children.

sampleRecalls.title: JCPenney Recalls Girls Puffer Jackets Due to Entanglement Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Arizona Jean Co. "Lilac Smoke" girls midweight puffer jackets sold in sizes 4-16 (XXS-XL). Item sub/lot number 302-0223, date code 08/20, RN #93677 and "Arizona Jean Co." are printed on the care label inside of the puffer jacket.
hazards: The puffer jackets have a drawstring located inside the lining of the bottom area of the garment. The drawstring can become entangled or caught on playground s…
injuries: No incidents or injuries have been reported.
products: Arizona Jean Co. "Lilac Smoke" Girls Midweight Puffer Jackets

sampleRecalls.title: JCPenney Recalls Okie Dokie Denim Patches Shortie Shorts Due to Choking Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Okie Dokie Denim Patches Shortie shorts. These jean shorts have a rainbow and a slice of watermelon patch on the front of each pocket with tan stitching along the seams. The shorts were sold in toddler sizes 2T through 5T. The item number 324-8191 and date code 12/17 are printed on a white tag on …
hazards: The patches on the shorts can detach, posing a choking hazard to young children.
products: Okie Dokie Denim Patches Shortie shorts

sampleRecalls.title: Nan Far Woodworking Recalls Rockland Furniture Round Cribs Due to Entrapment, Suffocation and Fall Hazards; Sold Exclusively at jcpenney
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Rockland Furniture round cribs with model number 343-8314. The model number is printed on a label located on the inner-lower portion of the crib rail. The cribs are 44 ½ inches in diameter and were sold in white, ebony and cherry finishes.
hazards: The crib's drop-side rails can malfunction, detach or otherwise fail. When this happens, the drop-side rail can fall out of position and create a space where a…

sampleRecalls.title: Nan Far Woodworking Recalls to Repair Drop-Side Cribs Due to Entrapment, Suffocation and Fall Hazards; Sold Exclusively at JcPenney
sampleRecalls.description: This recall includes Rockland Furniture brand drop-side cribs imported and sold by jcpenney from 2005 to 2008. The wooden cribs used plastic hardware to attach the drop side. "Rockland Furniture" and the model number can be found on a rectangular label located on the lower portion of the headboard panel. The following…
hazards: The cribs' drop sides can malfunction, detach or otherwise fail, causing part of the drop side to fall out of position, creating a space into which an infant o…
injuries: CPSC and the firms are aware of five incidents involving drop sides that malfunctioned or detached, including one report of a child who became entrapped and sustained minor scratches and bruises.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was taking a piece of paper from a register wrap stand when they tripped on a cord and fell to the floor, resulting in a right arm fracture.
An employee fell while getting up from a chair. She suffered a broken hip and was hospitalized.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to tripping over an object
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee tripped over a security cord and fell to the ground, resulting in a fractured hip.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slipping
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was walking in the break room and slipped on the floor. The employee hit his chin and was hospitalized for surgery.
An employee fell and fractured the right ankle and left tibia, requiring hospitalization.
